Chat Interface

Conversational AI component — the foundation for Monarch's LLM experiences.

This organism previews the direction for conversational AI interfaces within Monarch — inspired by the push-based, daily engagement model where the LLM proactively guides users through workflows.

Agent Chat

Monarch Intelligence

Your daily briefing agent

Good morning, Joe. ☀️ Here's your 2-minute briefing:

🔴

AK Design Group has stalled in Negotiation for 28 days. Last contact was a pricing objection.

📈

11 high-intent accounts detected overnight matching your ICP. Auto-enriched and scored.

✅

47 personalized emails sent to The Kitchen Source - Dallas. 12 opened, 3 clicked pricing.

8:00 AM

JY
What's the recommended next step for AK Design Group?

8:02 AM

Based on the pricing objection pattern, I recommend a two-step approach:

  1. 1. Send the ROI calculator showing projected savings over 24 months
  2. 2. Schedule an executive sponsor call within the week

8:02 AM

Design Decisions

• AI = square avatar, User = round avatar — instant visual differentiation

• Agent messages: elevated background with subtle border — distinct from user's gradient messages

• User messages: brand gradient — the user's voice is the hero

• Inline action buttons within AI responses — the agent can drive action, not just inform

• Suggested prompts as horizontal chips — reduces blank-page anxiety, guides daily interaction

• Typing indicator with bouncing dots — signals the agent is working

• Push-first model: the agent proactively briefs rather than waiting for questions

Cambria LLM Direction

This component establishes the visual foundation for the "5-minute daily" LLM experience. Key UX principles:

Give-to-Get Exchange

Employees answer the agent's daily questions and immediately get valuable insights back — not a one-way data extraction.

Push, Not Pull

The LLM proactively surfaces what matters. Users don't need to know what to ask — the agent leads the conversation.

Action, Not Information

Every AI response should enable action — send the email, schedule the call, update the deal — not just report status.

Progress Signals

Subtle progress indicators (streaks, completion, impact metrics) that reward daily engagement without gamification excess.
